name: Barrick Mining Corporation
description: Barrick Mining Corporation (formerly Barrick Gold) is one of the world's largest gold and copper mining companies. Operates six Tier One gold mines across Africa, Americas, and Asia Pacific.

quotes.speaker: Mark Bristow (CEO)
quotes.quote: By any measure, Barrick Gold Corporation today presents a standout value opportunity. Consensus NAV analysis shows Nevada and copper assets alone exceed our market cap — implying negative value for all our remaining Tier 1 and Tier 2 assets.
quotes.quote_th: ด้วยทุกมาตรวัด Barrick Gold Corporation ในวันนี้นำเสนอโอกาสสร้างมูลค่าที่โดดเด่น

quotes.speaker: Mark Bristow (CEO)
quotes.quote: We are focused on delivering sustainable long-term value by owning the best gold and copper assets managed by the best people. With gold becoming more important as a safe haven in a geopolitically uncertain world, and copper being as strategic as gold is precious.
quotes.quote_th: เรามุ่งมั่นสร้างมูลค่าระยะยาวอย่างยั่งยืนจากการถือครองสินทรัพย์ทองคำและทองแดงที่ดีที่สุด

quotes.speaker: Mark Bristow (CEO)
quotes.quote: Every one of our operations are independently viable, and Barrick's balance sheet is very solid. And so, with or without Loulo will not change our long-term plan, our five-year plan.
quotes.quote_th: ทุกหน่วยงานของเราสามารถดำรงอยู่ได้อย่างเป็นอิสระ และงบดุลของ Barrick แข็งแกร่งมาก

quotes.speaker: Mark Bristow (CEO)
quotes.quote: PV is a big value creator in Barrick. And again, you've seen I don't hesitate to clean up the portfolio at the appropriate time. And the best time is when you have higher commodity prices.
quotes.quote_th: Pueblo Viejo เป็นตัวสร้างมูลค่าสำคัญใน Barrick

quotes.speaker: Graham Shuttleworth (CFO)
quotes.quote: The key message is that Reko Diq transforms our copper production profile from 2029 onward, and Lumwana positions us as a top-tier copper producer well before that.
quotes.quote_th: ข้อความสำคัญคือ Reko Diq จะเปลี่ยนโฉมโปรไฟล์การผลิตทองแดงของเราจากปี 2029 เป็นต้นไป

roadmap.commitment: Potential IPO of North American gold assets (Nevada Gold Mines, Fourmile, related assets) — exploring structure and timing
Lumwana Super Pit Expansion: Major capex phase continuing — capex guided $600M+ in 2026, target to become top-tier copper producer
Incorporate Fourmile ultra-high-grade project into Nevada Gold Mines JV at fair market value
Search for permanent President and CEO to succeed Mark Bristow — interim leadership in place
Achieve 30%+ group-wide gold equivalent production growth by end of decade (2029-2030), anchored by Reko Diq, Lumwana, Pueblo Viejo, and Nevada
roadmap.follow_up: Construction ongoing, on schedule for 2028 production

roadmap.commitment: Resolve Mali/Loulo situation and restart production — arbitration filed, contingency plan for restart early March 2025 if employees released
roadmap.follow_up: Loulo-Gounkoto operations resumed in FY2025 after $559M settlement payment to Mali government in Q4 2025. Employees released. Royalty rate now 18% under 2023 Mining Code.

roadmap.commitment: Reko Diq Phase 1: Complete project financing by Q3 2025 and begin construction — production target end of 2028, cost $5.6–6B (100% basis)
roadmap.follow_up: Construction ongoing, on schedule for 2028 production

roadmap.commitment: Lumwana Super Pit Expansion: Deploy $600M capex in 2025 — feasibility complete, permits received, early works and long-lead fabrication underway
roadmap.follow_up: Review quarterly update for capex/permits/progress; update status and add evidence link.

roadmap.commitment: Pueblo Viejo: Achieve mid-600,000 oz attributable production in 2025, then exceed 800,000 oz from 2026 onward; El Naranjo tailings relocation complete by 2029
roadmap.follow_up: Review production guidance milestones; update status and add evidence link.

roadmap.commitment: Fourmile (Nevada): Complete pre-feasibility study within 3 years; allocate $78M in 2025 for study; grade significantly higher than adjacent Goldrush (~12g/t vs 6g/t)
roadmap.follow_up: Check study timeline/capex updates; update status and add evidence link.

financials.notes:
Barrick is a Canadian company (TSX: ABX, NYSE: B). Fiscal year ends December 31. Files 40-F (not 10-K) with SEC as a Canadian issuer. Unit = Billion USD except Gold Production (Moz) and AISC ($/oz).

Adjusted Net Earnings and Adjusted EPS exclude impairments, unrealized gains/losses, and other non-recurring items — management's preferred profitability measure.

FY2022 net earnings impacted by ~$700M impairment charges. FY2025 net earnings include $1,107M one-time acquisition/disposition gains — adjusted net earnings ($4.14B) better reflects underlying performance.

AISC (All-in Sustaining Cost per ounce): rising from $1,262 (2022) → $1,484 (2024) → $1,637 (2025) — cost inflation trend. Gold price realization: $1,803 (2022) → $1,948 (2023) → $2,397 (2024) → $3,501 (2025). Dramatic spread expansion drove FY2025 FCF tripling.

Note: FY2024 Gold Production corrected to 3.91 Moz (official 40-F) from earlier 4.05 estimate. FY2024 AISC corrected to $1,484/oz (official 40-F). FY2025 gold production decline (3.26 Moz) primarily due to Loulo-Gounkoto suspension and asset divestitures.

overview.business_model_summary: Barrick is a gold and copper mining company operating a portfolio of Tier One and Tier Two assets across four continents. Revenue is generated by mining and selling gold (primarily) and copper. The business model is capital-intensive — major upfront investment in mine construction, followed by decades of production at relatively stable unit costs. Profitability is determined by the spread between commodity prices and AISC. Under CEO Mark Bristow (former Randgold CEO), Barrick's defining characteristic is portfolio discipline: pruning non-core assets when prices are elevated, growing reserves organically rather than through overpriced M&A, and returning capital while self-funding growth.
overview.competitive_position: 2nd or 3rd largest gold miner globally by reserves and production volume. The Nevada Gold Mines JV with Newmont is the world's largest gold complex. Key differentiator: Tier-One asset concentration and operational expertise in technically complex jurisdictions. Primary competitors: Newmont Corporation (#1 globally), Agnico Eagle Mines, Gold Fields, AngloGold Ashanti.
moat_factors: Tier-1 asset portfolio with 10–30+ year mine lives
Operational expertise in complex jurisdictions (Randgold DNA)
Balance sheet self-funding growth without equity issuance
Mark Bristow's 25-year track record of value creation

segments.name: Nevada Gold Mines
segments.description: Joint venture with Newmont (Barrick 61.5%, Newmont 38.5%). World's largest gold mining complex. Barrick's most profitable and strategically important segment. Cortez is the crown jewel — highest-grade open-pit gold mine in North America.

segments.name: Africa & Middle East
segments.description: Loulo-Gounkoto (Mali 80%, SUSPENDED as of 2024), Kibali (DRC 45% JV), North Mara (Tanzania 84%), Bulyanhulu (Tanzania 84%), Tongon (Côte d'Ivoire 89.7%), Jabal Sayid copper (Saudi Arabia 50%). High-quality assets but elevated geopolitical risk.

segments.name: Copper (Global)
segments.description: Lumwana Super Pit (Zambia, 100%) — largest open-pit copper mine in Africa, undergoing $2B+ expansion. Zaldivar (Chile, 50% JV with Antofagasta). Jabal Sayid (Saudi Arabia, 50%). Reko Diq (Pakistan, 50% — under development, targeted 2028 production start, projected 38-year mine life).

segments.name: Latin America & Caribbean
segments.description: Pueblo Viejo (Dominican Republic, 60% JV with Newmont) — flagship. Expanding to 800Koz+ attributable from 2026 after tailings facility upgrade. Veladero (Argentina, 50% JV). High cost jurisdictions but large, long-life reserves.

segments.name: Asia Pacific & North America
segments.description: Porgera (Papua New Guinea, 47.5% JV — restarted 2024 after 4-year suspension). Hemlo (Ontario, Canada, 100%). Smaller combined contribution but Porgera restart adds meaningful production uplift.

timeline.title: Founded in Toronto
timeline.description: Barrick Gold Corporation founded by Peter Munk. Started as a small Canadian oil company that pivoted to gold mining.

timeline.title: Goldstrike Acquisition
timeline.description: Acquired the Goldstrike property in Nevada for $62M. This single acquisition became one of the most productive gold deposits in North American history and anchors the Nevada Gold Mines JV today.

timeline.title: Becomes World's #1 Producer
timeline.description: Barrick surpasses all rivals to become the world's largest gold mining company by production, driven by Nevada output.

timeline.title: Placer Dome Merger ($10.4B)
timeline.description: Acquired Placer Dome in a $10.4B deal — one of the largest mining M&A transactions of the decade. Added major assets including Cortez (Nevada), Pueblo Viejo, and Porgera.

timeline.title: Gold Price Crash & $8.7B Impairment
timeline.description: Gold price collapsed below $1,200/oz. Barrick recorded ~$8.7B in impairment charges and launched a major debt reduction / restructuring program. Revenue fell and confidence in management was shaken.

timeline.title: Randgold Merger ($18.3B)
timeline.description: All-share merger with London-listed Randgold Resources. Mark Bristow became President & CEO, bringing Randgold's operational discipline, African mining expertise, and reserve-focused philosophy to Barrick.

timeline.title: Nevada Gold Mines JV with Newmont
timeline.description: Combined all Nevada gold operations into a 61.5%/38.5% JV with Newmont, creating the world's largest gold mining complex. Saved ~$500M in annual synergies.

timeline.title: Reko Diq Agreement Signed
timeline.description: Reached landmark agreement with Pakistan government to revive the Reko Diq copper-gold project (Barrick 50%, Pakistan entities 50%). One of world's largest undeveloped copper-gold deposits.

timeline.title: Porgera Restart
timeline.description: Porgera gold mine (PNG) restarted after 4-year suspension following lease expiry dispute. Full production expected by 2025.

timeline.title: Record FCF & Reserve Growth
timeline.description: Free cash flow doubled to $1.32B. Operating CF $4.49B. Gold mineral reserves grew 23% to 89M oz. Returned ~$1.2B to shareholders. Gold averaged $2,397/oz.

timeline.title: Mali Crisis — Loulo Suspended
timeline.description: Malian government detained three Barrick employees. Loulo-Gounkoto mine (~400Koz/year) placed in care-and-maintenance. Arbitration filed. Resolved in Q4 2025 via $559M settlement.

timeline.title: Record Gold Price & Financial Performance
timeline.description: Gold averaged $3,432/oz (realized $3,501/oz) — a 44% increase over 2024. Revenue surged to $16.96B (+31%), FCF tripled to $3.87B. Company ended year with $2B net cash position.

timeline.title: Rebrand to Barrick Mining Corporation
timeline.description: Barrick Gold Corporation officially renamed to Barrick Mining Corporation, reflecting the strategic shift to become a gold AND copper producer. NYSE ticker changed from GOLD to B.

timeline.title: CEO Bristow Departure Announced
timeline.description: Mark Bristow, who led Barrick since the 2018 Randgold merger, announced departure. Search for permanent President & CEO underway. Bristow oversaw reserve growth, Nevada JV, and Reko Diq revival.

timeline.title: North America IPO Exploration
timeline.description: Barrick begins exploring potential IPO of North American gold assets (Nevada Gold Mines, Fourmile). Could be the largest strategic restructuring since the Nevada JV with Newmont in 2019.

management.name: Mark Bristow
management.title: President & CEO
management.background: Former CEO of Randgold Resources for 25 years. Geologist by training. Widely credited as one of the best capital allocators in mining. Designed the 2018 Barrick-Randgold merger and the 2019 Nevada Gold Mines JV. Known for mine visits, operational discipline, and blunt shareholder communication.

management.name: Graham Shuttleworth
management.title: Senior EVP & CFO
management.background: Former CFO of Randgold Resources. Joined with Bristow in the merger. Deep experience in mining finance across Africa and emerging markets. Focuses on balance sheet strength and disciplined capital allocation.

overview.bull_case: Gold price tailwinds from central bank buying, dedollarization trends, and persistent geopolitical instability support $2,000–$2,500+ gold long-term. Exceptional operational leverage — each $100/oz rise in gold price adds ~$400M FCF annually. Reko Diq copper project (Pakistan) provides decades of tier-one copper growth not yet priced into market cap. Organic reserve replacement at ~$10/oz is industry-leading and extends asset longevity. Balance sheet strong enough to self-fund all major capex without equity dilution. Bristow's track record on portfolio discipline and value creation is exceptional.
overview.bear_case: AISC creep ($1,262 → $1,390 over 3 years) narrows margin expansion narrative if gold plateaus. Mali/Loulo dispute (~10% of production) remains unresolved — prolonged suspension materially impairs 2025 targets. Reko Diq execution risk is the single biggest concern: $5.6-6B capex in Pakistan is the largest project in Barrick's history; delay, overrun, or political shift in Balochistan would be material. Gold price mean-reversion from $2,400+ would significantly compress FCF and re-rate the stock.

corporate_structure.name: Barrick Mining Corp (TSX: ABX / NYSE: GOLD)

key_risks.description: Loulo-Gounkoto (Mali, 80% Barrick) contributes ~400Koz/year (~10% of attributable gold production). Three Barrick employees detained by Malian government since end-2024. Mine in care-and-maintenance. No timeline for resolution.
key_risks.mitigation: Arbitration filed under ICSID rules. Company states balance sheet can absorb impact. Mali operations excluded from 2025 production guidance.

key_risks.description: Largest single capex commitment in Barrick history ($5.6-6B, Barrick 50%). Located in Balochistan, Pakistan — historically challenging jurisdiction. Project financing not yet signed as of Q4 2024 (targeting Q3 2025). Production target end-2028.
key_risks.mitigation: 50% JV with Pakistani government entities aligns interests. Board conditionally approved bankable feasibility. Multiple international DFI lenders engaged.

key_risks.description: Revenue and FCF highly correlated to spot gold price. AISC ~$1,390/oz. A $100/oz gold price decline reduces annual revenue ~$400M. Return to $1,800/oz gold would eliminate most FCF and require significant capex prioritization.
key_risks.mitigation: No gold hedging (management philosophy: full exposure to gold). Copper diversification partially offsets — ~16% of revenue. Geographic diversification provides operational stability.

key_risks.description: AISC rose from $1,262/oz (2022) to $1,390/oz (2024) — a $128/oz increase in 2 years driven by labor, energy, and reagent inflation. Industry-wide trend. If gold price plateaus, margin compression accelerates.
key_risks.mitigation: Lumwana Super Pit and Pueblo Viejo expansions designed to lower unit costs at key operations. Ongoing operational efficiency programs.

key_risks.description: ~40-50% of production from Mali, DRC, Tanzania, Pakistan, PNG — all elevated-risk jurisdictions. Regulatory changes, resource nationalism, or instability can disrupt operations with limited warning.
key_risks.mitigation: Randgold DNA: Barrick has decades of operating expertise in complex African/emerging market jurisdictions. Local partnership and community engagement model proven effective historically.
